2606.16989.title: Stable Menus of Public Goods: AI-Enabled Progress
2606.16989.paper_summary: Using an open problem from the EC 2025 paper "Stable Menus of Public Goods" as a testbed, we conduct experiments to understand the effectiveness of different AI-for-EconCS research workflows. Specifically, we study three questions: Does providing human intuition in the prompt help? Does automated multi-turn interaction help? And, does an LLM outperform a first-year PhD student? Regarding the first two questions, we provide evidence for the following workflow suggestions: (1) prompting with human intuition can encourage the LLM to have better "taste", (2) multi-turn workflows help when the pipeline encourages "ambitious" steps. Regarding the third question, using an unpublished manuscript written by the paper's senior authors prior to collaborating with the first-year PhD student, we compare the effectiveness of the LLM with that of the first-year PhD student, and find that the LLM is slightly less effective.
2606.16989.paper_summary_zh: 使用來自 EC 2025 論文《公共物品的穩定菜單》的開放問題作為測試平台，我們進行實驗以了解不同 AI-for-EconCS 研究工作流程的有效性。<lb/>具體而言，我們研究三個問題：提供人類直覺的提示是否有幫助？自動化的多輪互動是否有幫助？以及，LLM 是否超越一名一年級博士生？<lb/>關於前兩個問題，我們提供以下工作流程建議的證據：（1）使用人類直覺提示可以鼓勵 LLM 擁有更好的「品味」，（2）當流程鼓勵「雄心勃勃」的步驟時，多輪工作流程會有所幫助。<lb/>關於第三個問題，我們使用一篇未發表的手稿，該手稿是論文的資深作者在與一年級博士生合作之前撰寫的，並比較 LLM 與一年級博士生的有效性，發現 LLM 的效果略遜一籌。
2606.16989.author: Sara Fish et.al.
